qa_pairs.question: What was the duration of the scheduled electrical maintenance for Béluga, Narval, and Juno (non-HA) in April 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The scheduled electrical maintenance for Béluga, Narval, and Juno (non-HA) lasted one day, from April 30, 2025, at 12:00 PM (EDT) until May 1, 2025, at 12:00 PM (EDT).

qa_pairs.question: Which systems were shut down during the electrical maintenance from April 30 to May 1, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The Béluga and Narval compute nodes were shut down, along with cloud instances in the Juno cloud's non-high availability zone.

qa_pairs.question: Were there any parts of the Béluga and Juno systems unaffected by the April 30 – May 1, 2025, electrical maintenance?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Béluga cloud instances and Juno cloud instances in the high availability zone were not affected by these interruptions.

qa_pairs.question: Could users access storage on Béluga and Narval during the April 30, 2025, outage?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Béluga and Narval storage remained accessible through Globus and the login nodes of each cluster during the outage.

qa_pairs.question: What happened to jobs scheduled to finish after 12:00 PM on April 30, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Jobs scheduled to finish after 12:00 PM on April 30 remained queued until the clusters were back online.

qa_pairs.question: Why was the Cedar cluster unavailable from March 31 to April 2, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar cluster was unavailable for a planned outage to perform power modifications as part of preparations for bringing new equipment online.

qa_pairs.question: What was the impact on users during the Cedar cluster outage from March 31 to April 2, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Users were unable to log in or run jobs on the Cedar cluster. Any jobs running at the time were terminated and had to be re-submitted once the cluster was back online.

qa_pairs.question: Did the Cedar Cloud remain operational during the March 31 to April 2, 2025, outage?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Cedar Cloud remained operational during this period.

qa_pairs.question: What was the original and extended duration of the Graham cluster outage for data centre renovations?
qa_pairs.answer: The Graham cluster outage was originally scheduled from December 7, 2024, to January 3, 2025, but was extended to February 24, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What services were unavailable during the Graham cluster outage starting December 7, 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: All Graham cluster services, storage, and cloud services were entirely unavailable during this outage.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find updated information about the extended Graham outage?
qa_pairs.answer: For updated information, users can see https://status.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What caused the temporary reduction in capacity for Béluga and Narval from January 13 to February 14, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The temporary reduction was due to performance and stability tests on Rorqual.

qa_pairs.question: Which compute nodes were affected during the Béluga and Narval temporary reduction starting January 13, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: All Béluga compute nodes and about half of Narval compute nodes were shut down.

qa_pairs.question: What was the initial schedule for the Béluga and Narval compute node shutdown in January 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The initial shutdown was from 8 a.m. on January 13 until 12 p.m. (noon) on January 31, 2025 (EST).

qa_pairs.question: Were login nodes and data access available during the Béluga and Narval temporary reduction?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, login nodes and data access remained operational.

qa_pairs.question: What happened to Béluga Storage during the temporary reduction in January 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: During the shutdown time, Béluga Storage (/lustre01, /lustre02, /lustre03, /lustre04 of Beluga) was mounted to Narval.

qa_pairs.question: Were Béluga and Juno cloud instances affected by the temporary reduction starting January 13, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Béluga and Juno cloud instances were unaffected.

qa_pairs.question: When were Béluga and Narval expected to return to 100% capacity after the temporary reduction?
qa_pairs.answer: Béluga and Narval were expected to be back to 100% capacity on February 14, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: Why were Niagara and Mist compute nodes shut down on January 22, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Niagara and Mist compute nodes were shut down to support ongoing system improvements and integration with the new Trillium system.

qa_pairs.question: What was the timeframe for the Niagara and Mist compute node shutdown on January 22, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The shutdown was from 8 AM to 5 PM EST on January 22, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: Which services remained available during the Niagara and Mist outage on January 22, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The login nodes, file systems, and the HPSS system remained available.

qa_pairs.question: What happened to jobs submitted during the Niagara and Mist maintenance on January 22, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The scheduler held jobs that were submitted until the maintenance had finished.

qa_pairs.question: What was the reason for the Cedar compute cluster shutdown from January 13 to January 21, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar compute cluster was shut down in preparation for infrastructure renewal.

qa_pairs.question: What action was recommended for users with important data on the Cedar /scratch filesystem during the January 2025 outage?
qa_pairs.answer: Users were asked to move any important data immediately to their /project, /nearline, or /home directory as the /scratch filesystem was being migrated.

qa_pairs.question: Was Cedar cloud affected by the compute cluster shutdown from January 13 to January 21, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Cedar cloud remained operational during this period.
